hypothesis: An ensemble of LLM agents with systematically varied sampling temperatures (e.g., 0.2, 0.5, 0.8) and different per-agent experimental time budgets whose results are synthesized by a supervising aggregator agent will, for a fixed total simulated experimental time, identify a higher fraction of the true underlying ALP reaction mechanisms (higher recall) and produce a lower rate of incorrect rule attributions (lower false positive rate) than a single LLM agent using the same total experimental budget.
